one_line_thesis: Post-merger diagnostics platform (Quidel + Ortho) with improving adjusted margins but declining revenue — integration and non-respiratory growth must prove out before this is a compounder again.

deep_dive.executive_summary: QuidelOrtho is a leveraged diagnostics turnaround. We value it with a segment lens (recurring core vs volatile POC vs LEX option), not a single blended multiple. Marvin base: 18.05% per year at $13.79 on filings-based owner cash and synthesis weights. Proof-first component schedule base economic value ~$8.68/share (Labs+IH engine $7.58, options $1.10, near-term cash claim $1.65, downside reserve −$1.65). Segment sum-of-parts (status quo): ~$49/share at peer-discounted multiples. Post-POC sale scenario (~$1.5B proceeds, base multiples): ~$83/share. McIntyre Partnerships normalization is cont...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

options.falsifier: Primary evidence shows that the economic claim, cash conversion, ownership, or required capital is materially worse than this low case.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: Binary and milestone-driven assets
failure_modes: pipeline value counted in both revenue forecast and option schedule

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of QDEL, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Derived from inputs.shares_millions in the pre-existing valuation.json; [HUMAN REVIEW] confirm against the latest primary filing.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

timeline.label: Operating income moved -53.1% versus the prior period (1,960.9 to 919.2).
Basic EPS moved -45.4% versus the prior period (30.5 to 16.7).
Financial Times reported QuidelOrtho exploring ~$1.5B sale of point-of-care unit to cut merger debt; unconfirmed catalyst.
Q1 FY2026 earnings: revenue $619.8M (−10.5% YoY); FY26 guide flat sales $2.70–2.75B and EBITDA $615–630M.
FY2025 10-K filed: revenue $2,730.2M (−1.9% YoY); adj EBITDA $597M (+240 bps margin); net debt ~$2.38B.
CEO Brian Blaser open-market purchase ~23,500 shares (~$501K); insider buying cluster through Feb 2026.
U.S. donor screening business exit in progress; FY2025 revenue $52.6M (−54% YoY).
LEX / Savanna molecular platform launch investment; company guided ~$50M launch drag for 2026.
Post-COVID respiratory normalization continued; POC revenue volatility persists.
Quidel and Ortho Clinical Diagnostics merger closed; combined leverage ~$3.8B merger debt overhang.
Post-merger leadership integration; Brian Blaser CEO through refresh period.
Quidel acquired Ortho Clinical Diagnostics for ~$3.8B, creating QuidelOrtho and heavy term debt.

industry.structure: Consolidated diagnostics oligopoly (Abbott, Roche, Danaher, Siemens) with high recurring consumable mix on installed instruments; reimbursement and hospital budget pressure cap pricing.
industry.share_shift: Post-COVID POC respiratory volumes normalized down; central lab and transfusion franchises steadier; molecular platforms (LEX, GeneXpert) compete for hospital capital budgets.
industry.trend: Margin expansion via cost programs offsetting flat revenue; strategic portfolio simplification (POC sale rumors, donor screening exit) to address merger leverage.

top_claims.claim: Leadership / governance on watch. QuidelOrtho CFO Micah Young Receives 356,555 Stock Units - Stock Titan
Our investment in QDEL is a significantly concentrated position, which makes market comparisons less useful at present.

QuidelOrtho is a leveraged diagnostics turnaround. We value it with a segment lens (recurring core vs volatile POC vs LEX option), not a single blended multiple. Marvin base: 15.4% per year at $13.79 on $1.65/share filings-based owner cash, 9 times exit, 2.5% / 2% growth. Segment sum-of-parts (status quo): ~$49/share a
We expect about **15.4%** per year at **$13.79** on filings-based owner cash, with segment SOTP upside to **~$49/share** (status quo) or **~$83/share** (post-POC at base multiples).

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: POC sale fails or prices below $1.0B, leaving leverage and flu/China headwinds unresolved.
Revenue decline persists beyond FY2026 guide
China reimbursement cuts deeper than modeled

factors.title: QuidelOrtho CFO Micah Young Receives 356,555 Stock Units - Stock Titan
